multiplechoiceresponse.label: A policymaker argues that predictive policing outcomes cannot be biased because the model simply reflects historical arrest data. According to Lecture 2, what is the main flaw in this argument?

choicegroup.choice: Historical data may contain measurement error and reporting noise
Arrest data captures only short-term crime trends
Historical data itself may already encode the effects of past feedback loops
The model must explicitly include protected attributes to detect bias

div.p: Datasets created through human decision‑making often already carry the imprint of earlier biases and interventions. Past policing strategies influence where arrests occur, which then shapes future training data. Treating historical data as neutral ignores the possibility that disparities were amplified over time through repeated feedback loops. Measurement noise or feature inclusion alone does not address this structural issue.
